title: elizaOS/eliza: 500 commits
summary: fix, feature, other, docs — docs(lifeops): record round 20 (decomposed-view e2e executed green, desktop + mobile viewport); test(app): run decomposed-view e2e at mobile (Pixel 7) viewport too; fix(app): decomposed-view e2e green across all 8 views (web/linux lane)
consider: 479 human-authored commits observed in the week.
479 non-bot, non-admin commits remain after basic filtering.
Documentation or launch/readout movement is present.
do_not_consider: 21 bot/generated commits should not count as product momentum.
High-volume week: promote only if the examples summarize a meaningful phase, not raw activity volume.
review_questions: Is this the team's real project repo for this period?
Do the example commit subjects describe product/research movement rather than generated churn?
Would this card add information not already captured by profile, transcript, or calendar timeline items?
